_comment: Three messages, two of which produce nothing. The point of the fixture is that triage's first job is deciding what NOT to surface: only the message containing a direct ask reaches the Inbox. The other two are a broadcast and a thread where Sandip is cc'd but nobody is waiting on him.

email.message.subject: Re: Pricing v2 — need your read before Thursday
email.message.body:
Sandip, the deck is in decent shape but I'm stuck on the middle tier.

Could you look at slides 8-11 and tell me whether the usage-based cut holds up? My worry is that it reads clean to us and looks arbitrary to a buyer who hasn't been in these conversations.

Also, what's your take on keeping the annual discount at 20%? Finance is pushing for 15 and I'd rather not fight it without a reason.

I need to send this to the board by end of day Thursday, so any time before Wednesday evening works.

email.message.subject: Resolved: Elevated error rates in iad1
email.message.body: This incident has been resolved. Between 06:12 and 06:48 UTC, a subset of deployments in iad1 returned elevated 5xx responses. No action is required.

email.message.subject: Notes from Tuesday standup
email.message.body:
Quick notes for anyone who missed it.

The migration is done and the old workers are drained. Dana is picking up the alerting cleanup this week. We agreed to push the retro to Monday so the whole team can make it.

Nothing blocking. Copying Sandip for visibility.
